Fix response parsing: use recursive JSON parser for Gemini response

The regex text extractor "(.-)"%s*[,}%]] was truncating nested JSON
responses. String values like "subject" in masks have their escaped
closing \" followed by , or }, which triggered premature regex match.

Now parses the full Gemini response body with the recursive descent
parser and navigates to candidates[1].content.parts[1].text to get
properly unescaped text content. Falls back to regex only if the
structured parse fails.

- Add soft-delete (deletedAt/deletedById) and AdminActionLog table to schema
- Split Prisma client into filtered (prisma) and raw (prismaAdmin) for soft-delete

- Update webhook handler to skip soft-deleted users
- Add admin UI with actions dropdown, confirmation dialogs, status filters

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: streaming voice pipeline with barge-in support (#454)

* feat: stream LLM→TTS→Audio concurrently for lower voice latency

Previously the voice pipeline was fully sequential: LLM finishes
entirely, then TTS synthesizes all sentences, then audio plays
everything. Now the LLM runs in a background thread and sends
sentences to the main thread as they form. Each sentence is
synthesized and pushed to a continuous streaming audio output
immediately, so the user hears the first sentence while the LLM
is still generating the rest.

Key changes:
- Add StreamingPlayback to AudioOutput (shared VecDeque buffer,
  continuous cpal stream, no gaps between sentence chunks)
- LLM runs in a spawned thread with sentence boundary detection
  in the on_token callback
- TTS synthesizes per-sentence on the main thread and pushes
  audio incrementally
- Transcript emitted as soon as LLM finishes, even if audio is
  still playing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: strip markdown from TTS output and simplify voice system prompt

LLM responses sometimes contain markdown formatting (bold, backticks,
etc.) which TTS reads literally as "asterisk asterisk". Add
strip_markdown() to clean formatting before synthesis, and simplify
the system prompt to just set the voice chat context.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add barge-in support for voice mode (headphones required)

Users can now interrupt the assistant mid-response by speaking.
The mic stays open during TTS playback, and VAD monitors for
speech using crossbeam select! on both the sentence channel and
mic channel simultaneously.

On barge-in: cancels audio playback, saves partial response with
[interrupted] marker in conversation history, and feeds the
captured speech back into the VAD loop to collect the full
utterance before processing.

Also adds cancel() and is_done() to StreamingPlayback, and
removes audio_in mute/unmute (mic stays open).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prevent false barge-in by sealing playback before drain loop

The drain loop never signaled done_writing, so is_done() never
returned true. The loop kept running until ambient noise falsely
triggered the VAD, which cancelled playback mid-sentence. Fix by
calling seal() to signal no more audio before entering the drain
loop.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: require 300ms sustained speech to confirm barge-in

A single VAD SpeechStart from a noise spike was enough to cancel
playback mid-sentence. Now we accumulate 300ms of audio after
SpeechStart and only trigger barge-in if speech is sustained
(SpeechEnd doesn't fire during the confirmation window).

Also: don't reset VAD on barge-in (preserves triggered state for
ongoing speech), and skip the 300ms settle delay for faster
interruption response.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(core): add SCPID wire types and challenge generation (closes #1053) (#1068)

* feat(core): add SCPID wire types and challenge generation (closes #1053)

Implements the SCPID authentication protocol types and challenge
generation per spec §3.11:

- ScpIdChallenge, ScpIdResponse, ScpIdAuthentication wire types
- ScpIdError with SCP-IDENT-103x error codes
- scpid_challenge() with CSPRNG nonce, TTL/audience validation
- Hex serde for [u8; 32] (nonce) and [u8; 64] (signature) fields
- 11 tests including golden vector verified against Python

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(core): address review findings — ms timestamps, protocol validation, zero TTL, error codes (closes #1053)

- Finding 0: Change timestamps from seconds to milliseconds per §3.11.2
  (issued_at, expires_at, signed_at). Update golden vector hash.
- Finding 1: Add serde deserialize_with on protocol field to reject
  invalid protocol versions on deserialization for both ScpIdChallenge
  and ScpIdResponse.
- Finding 2: Reject zero TTL (produces expires_at == issued_at).
- Finding 4: Update spec §3.11.8 to use ScpIdError instead of
  IdentityError — keeps protocol-level auth errors separate from
  identity-layer concerns. Add SCP-IDENT-1037/1038 to error table.
- Finding 5: Add error codes SCP-IDENT-1037 (SigningFailed) and
  SCP-IDENT-1038 (InvalidInput) to Display strings.
- Finding 8: Use as_millis() for TTL computation, eliminating
  sub-second precision truncation. Boundary test updated to
  Duration::from_millis(300_001).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(core): fix test signature length and add protocol to spec structs

Fix Ed25519 signature hex string in test_invalid_protocol_rejected_on_response_deser
from 136 chars (68 bytes) to correct 128 chars (64 bytes). Add missing
protocol field to ScpIdChallenge and ScpIdResponse struct definitions
in §3.11.2 and §3.11.3 of the identity spec.

commit.message:
fix(core): align bridge credential HKDF with §12.11 key isolation (closes #1065) (#1066)

* fix(core): align bridge credential HKDF with §12.11 key isolation (closes #1065)

The derive_credential_key function had three parameter mismatches vs
spec §12.11.1 Phase 2:

- IKM: was operator identity key, now per-bridge random secret
  (bridge_credential_key: &[u8; 32])
- Salt: was dynamic bridge_id||credential_type, now fixed
  SHA-256("SCP-BRIDGE-CREDENTIAL-V1") hash
- Info: was constant b"scp-bridge-credential-v1", now
  "scp-bridge-credential:{bridge_id}" for per-bridge isolation

Updated BridgeCredentialStore trait, InMemoryCredentialStore, and
OAuthCredentialManager to use the new signature. Added tests for
key isolation across different IKM values.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): align credential key derivation formatting with rustfmt

Fix formatting for method chaining in derive_credential_key function
to meet cargo fmt requirements.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(core): address review findings — CSPRNG provisioning, custody storage, revoke cleanup (closes #1065)

- Finding 1: Add generate_bridge_credential_key() with CSPRNG (OsRng)
- Finding 2: Add store/get/delete_bridge_credential_key to trait + InMemoryCredentialStore
- Finding 3: revoke() now destroys bridge_credential_key via delete_bridge_credential_key
- Finding 6: Cache HKDF salt in LazyLock (no recomputation per call)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(core): wrap generate_bridge_credential_key in Zeroizing, update stale PRD

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(core): address PR review — Zeroizing stack copy, KeyNotFound error variant

1. Fill random bytes directly into the Zeroizing wrapper in
   generate_bridge_credential_key() to avoid leaving a plain [u8; 32]
   copy on the stack (Copy semantics).

2. Add CredentialError::KeyNotFound { bridge_id } variant for missing
   bridge credential keys instead of reusing NotFound with a synthetic
   CredentialType::Custom("bridge_credential_key") — keys are not
   credentials and deserve distinct error semantics.

Implements the 6-step compromise recovery protocol from spec §9.12:
- CompromiseRecoveryOrchestrator with 3-tier key rotation (agent/active/identity)
- RecoveryBackend trait for platform-specific MLS, UCAN, KeyPackage, notification, PSK ops

  recovery_send_notification instead of only logging

commit.message:
fix(wasm): restrict enum parsing to lowercase-only matching reference bridge (closes #1029) (#1067)

Remove PascalCase arms from SourceType::from_str, ContextState::from_str,
and MemoryScope::from_str in the WASM bridge provenance module. The WASM
bridge was the only bridge accepting both PascalCase and lowercase, while
the reference PyO3 bridge and NAPI bridge accept only lowercase/snake_case.
This caused a cross-bridge asymmetry where code tested against WASM with
PascalCase inputs would silently fail against NAPI.

Updates all WASM tests to use lowercase, converts PascalCase acceptance
tests in tests_native to rejection tests, and updates doc comments to
advertise lowercase-only values.

fix(ffi): wire production providers, NotConfiguredTransport, explicit init (closes #501) (#1069)

* fix(ffi): wire NotConfiguredTransportProvider, replace noop_key_resolver, add MLS test (#501)

Replace silent-success providers with fail-loud alternatives across all 3
FFI bridges (PyO3, NAPI, UniFFI):

- Transport: NotConfiguredTransportProvider returns descriptive errors for
  publish/send/delete and is_connected()=false, instead of
  LocalTransportProvider which silently succeeded on all ops.
- Key resolver: not_configured_key_resolver logs error-level on every
  lookup instead of warn-once, making missing key resolution visible per
  DID rather than silently skipping vote verification.
- Test: context_create_establishes_mls_group in e2e_bridge.rs verifies the
  full bridge path (identity create -> context create -> ContextManager
  state populated with creator as admin member).
- Docs: Updated CLAUDE.md for scp-ffi and scp-ffi-napi to reflect
  production providers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ffi): require explicit ContextManager init in NAPI and UniFFI bridges

Match the PyO3 bridge pattern: context_manager() now returns an error if
the manager has not been initialized via init_context_manager() (called
from context_create). This prevents silently auto-initializing with
potentially invalid state.

For UniFFI-exported functions that return non-Result types (bool, Vec,
Option, ()), a context_manager_or_init() helper auto-initializes with
a warning log, since these function signatures cannot propagate errors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(uniffi): panic instead of silent auto-init in context_manager_or_init (#501)

Replace the silent auto-initialization fallback in context_manager_or_init()
with a descriptive panic. The previous behavior silently created a
ContextManager with no-op providers when called before context_create(),
contradicting #501's goal of eliminating silent degradation.

The panic message directs callers to call context_create() first. This is
appropriate because: (1) all callers are infallible UniFFI exports for
membership/broadcast/event/TTL queries that only make sense after a context
exists, and (2) Swift/Kotlin have exception handling that surfaces the panic
as a catchable error.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(core): remove transport connectivity check from create_context (#501)

Context creation is a local operation (MLS group init, sender key
generation, event log bootstrap) and should not require transport
connectivity. The is_connected() check in Phase 1 validation caused
ALL context creation to fail when using NotConfiguredTransportProvider,
which is the default before transport_connect() is called.

Transport errors are now surfaced naturally at Phase 2 step 5
(publish_context) where they belong. Also fixes 9 stale "infallible"
doc comments in the NAPI bridge that now call context_manager()?.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(core): make publish_context best-effort in create_context (#501)

Context creation is a local operation — the context is fully functional
even without relay publication. Previously, NotConfiguredTransportProvider
returned Err(TransportFailed) from publish_context, which caused
create_context to roll back all locally-created state (MLS group, sender
key, event log). Now publish errors are logged as warnings and the context
proceeds to Active state.

- Catch transport errors at Phase 2 step 5 and log warning instead of failing
- Update create_context_succeeds_when_transport_disconnected test to use
  NotConfiguredTransportProvider (verifies actual production path)
- Rename create_context_rollback_on_publish_failure to

- Rename create_context_transport_failure_returns_transport_error to

- Fix stale comment in scp-ffi runtime.rs about is_connected() check

feat(core): implement app sandboxing with ScopedHandle and CapabilityDeclaration (closes #595) (#1062)

* feat(core): implement app sandboxing with ScopedHandle and CapabilityDeclaration (closes #595)

Implements spec sections 8.4.1 (Capability Declaration Wire Format) and
8.4.2 (SDK-Level Enforcement) with full app sandboxing system:

Core types (scp-core):
- CapabilityDeclaration: wire format with Ed25519 JCS signature
- ScopedHandle: capability-restricted context handle with per-capability
  check methods and no-escalation guarantee
- SandboxError: typed error hierarchy
- validate_declaration(): all-or-nothing bind-time validation against
  context ceiling and role capabilities
- AppBound/AppUnbound ContextEvent variants for event log recording
- sign_declaration() and declaration_content_hash() helpers

FFI bridges (all 4):
- PyO3: py_validate_capability_declaration, py_check_scoped_capability
- NAPI: validateCapabilityDeclaration, checkScopedCapability
- UniFFI: sandbox_validate_declaration, sandbox_check_capability
- WASM: sandbox_validate_declaration, sandbox_check_capability

SDK wrappers (all 4):
- Python: ScopedHandle class, validate_capability_declaration()
- TypeScript: ScopedHandle class, validateCapabilityDeclaration()
- Swift: ScopedHandle struct, validateCapabilityDeclaration()
- Kotlin: ScopedHandle data class, DeclarationValidationResult

101 tests covering serialization roundtrips, signature verification,
ceiling validation, role intersection, ScopedHandle enforcement for
every capability, no-escalation guarantee, and event log recording.

dropping all event log entries. This violated issue #484's acceptance
criterion that event log entries are persisted on each append.

backed by ProtocolRepositoryEventLogBridge over an encrypted in-memory
storage provider (EncryptingAdapter<InMemoryStorage>). This matches the
pattern available via ContextManagerBuilder::storage() in scp-core.

SDK integration concern — consumers needing durability should provide a

commit.message:
Fix Layout/DefEndAlignment: 1 FP and 3 FN from minified code and parenthesized defs

RuboCop's on_def handler aligns end with the def keyword in BOTH modes
(start_of_line and def). The start_of_line vs def distinction only applies
in on_send for modifier methods (e.g., private def foo). nitrocop was
always using line-start alignment in start_of_line mode, causing:

FP: Minified code like `class H<Hash;def method_missing...end` where end
aligns with def keyword (col 14) but not with line start (col 0).

FN: Non-modifier mid-line defs like `false && def`, `protected (def`,
and `module X;def` where end at col 0 matched line start but not the
def keyword column.

Fix: Detect whether the text before def on the same line is a modifier
prefix (identifiers + whitespace only). For non-modifier mid-line defs,
align end with the def keyword instead of the line start.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix Layout/EmptyLinesAroundBlockBody: 1 FP and 6 FN from line continuation and brace blocks

FP: backslash line continuation before `do` (e.g. `method(arg) \\\n  do |x|`)
was incorrectly flagging the blank line after `do` as extra. RuboCop uses
`send_node.last_line` as the reference line, so the `do` on a continuation
line is considered part of the block opener. Fix: walk backward through
`\\`-continued lines to find the effective first line.

FN: lambda brace/do blocks (`-> (a) {`, `-> do`) were not checked because
the cop only visited BLOCK_NODE. Added LAMBDA_NODE to interested_node_types
and extracted opening/closing offsets from both node types.

feat(transport): implement CoverTrafficSender for NativeRelayAdapter (closes #490) (#1047)

* fix(transport): address review findings — resource leak, API ergonomics, test coverage (#490)

Finding 3 (MAJOR): Add CancellationToken to NativeRelayAdapter with Drop
impl that cancels the cover traffic background task, preventing resource
leaks from the Arc cycle between the task and adapter.

Finding 4 (MAJOR): Change start_cover_traffic to take &self instead of
&Arc<Self>. NativeRelayClient is now Clone (all fields are Arc-wrapped),
so the background task clones the client directly. No Arc<Self> wrapping
required by the caller.

Finding 6 (MINOR): Add integration tests that exercise the real
NativeRelayAdapter::send_cover_traffic() against a local relay server,
plus a drop_cancels_cover_traffic_task test verifying cleanup.

commit.message:
Fix Layout/SpaceInsideReferenceBrackets: 1 FN from multiline bracket ref with trailing space

The whole-node multiline check was skipping IndexOperatorWriteNode when the
RHS value was on a different line, even though the brackets were single-line.
RuboCop's on_send only sees the inner [] send node, not the outer compound
assignment. Restrict the whole-node multiline skip to CallNode only.

commit.message:
fix(ffi): swap arguments in block_broadcast_subscriber across bridges (closes #1003) (#1045)

* fix(ffi): swap argument order in block_broadcast_subscriber — all bridges passed subscriber as author_did (closes #1003)

All three non-WASM FFI bridges (PyO3, napi-rs, UniFFI) were calling
ContextManager::block_broadcast_subscriber with subscriber and blocker
arguments reversed, passing the subscriber DID as author_did and the
blocker DID as subscriber_did.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ffi): add regression test and align WASM parameter convention (#1003)

Add a regression test verifying that block_broadcast_subscriber records
the blocker DID as author_did in both BlockResult and MemberBlocked
event (issue #1003 acceptance criterion).

Align WASM bridge parameter order for block_broadcast_subscriber and
unblock_broadcast_subscriber with scp-core convention: author/blocker
first, subscriber second. The previous reversed order was the same
convention mismatch that caused the original bug in the other bridges.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
